newsapi.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become - TechCrunch

bbc.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
bbc.description: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

bbc.title: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
bbc.description: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

bbc.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
bbc.description: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

bbc.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
bbc.description: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

bbc.title: Iran begins public mourning for Ayatollah killed in February
bbc.description: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla from Friday ahead of days-long funeral events.

bbc.title: German row over plan for workers to need sick note on first day of illness
bbc.description: A doctors' group says it "borders on madness" that patients will have to obtain the note in person.

bbc.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
bbc.description: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.

bbc.title: Stand-up comic held for jokes about Erdoğan and Islam in Turkey crackdown
bbc.description: Deniz Göktaş is regarded as one of Turkey's most popular comedians and is accused of "inciting hatred and hostility" on stage.

bbc.title: 'Start work at 11' - but will other bosses be as flexible over England's 1am match?
bbc.description: Employers are being urged to use their "common sense" to allow staff to work flexibly where they can.

bbc.title: No-gift policy for Taylor Swift, but how much should you give at a wedding?
bbc.description: Wedding lists are being replaced by cash requests, but guests are divided over how much to give.

bbc.title: Tackle workplace sickness to unlock hidden growth, former John Lewis boss says
bbc.description: More than 250 of the UK's biggest employers have signed up to his 'Get Britain Working' taskforce.

bbc.title: Burnham says there is some room for movement on tax
bbc.description: But the likely next PM says he will stick to Labour's pledges to not raise VAT, income tax or national insurance.

bbc.title: Pubs allowed to stay open until 5am on Monday for England Mexico match
bbc.description: The government had initially said it would not relax licensing laws further for the World Cup.

bbc.title: AI is 'not smart' so what's next in artificial intelligence?
bbc.description: Leading AI researcher Yan LeCun has a start-up which is developing a more flexible AI system.

bbc.title: 'Less than 10% sheep': How millions may have unknowingly eaten goat, skin and fat kebabs
bbc.description: Millions are likely to have eaten "lamb" kebabs that were actually made with goat, skin and fat.

bbc.title: Instagram running ads promoting child sexual abuse material in India, BBC finds
bbc.description: The ads use terms including “rape” and “child video” and link to content on the messaging app Telegram.
